name: qdrant
description: Manage Qdrant vector database collections and search or upsert points

envVars.name: QDRANT_API_KEY
envVars.description: Qdrant database API key for your cluster

tools.name: List Collections
tools.description: List all collections in the Qdrant cluster

clusterHost.description: Qdrant cluster hostname without scheme or port (the :6333 REST port is added automatically), e.g. xyz-example.eu-central-1-0.aws.cloud.qdrant.io

tools.name: Get Collection Info
tools.description: Get a collection's configuration, vector parameters, and point count

clusterHost.description: Qdrant cluster hostname without scheme or port (the :6333 REST port is added automatically), e.g. xyz-example.eu-central-1-0.aws.cloud.qdrant.io

tools.name: Search Points
tools.description: Search a collection for the points nearest to a query vector

clusterHost.description: Qdrant cluster hostname without scheme or port (the :6333 REST port is added automatically), e.g. xyz-example.eu-central-1-0.aws.cloud.qdrant.io

vector.description: Query vector values (must match the collection's vector size)

limit.description: Number of nearest points to return

score_threshold.description: Exclude results with a similarity score below this value

tools.name: Upsert Points
tools.description: Insert or update points (vectors with payloads) in a collection

clusterHost.description: Qdrant cluster hostname without scheme or port (the :6333 REST port is added automatically), e.g. xyz-example.eu-central-1-0.aws.cloud.qdrant.io

wait.description: Wait until the operation is committed before responding

prompts.title: List my collections
prompts.prompt: List the collections in my Qdrant cluster and show each one's vector size and point count.

prompts.title: Search a collection
prompts.prompt: Search my Qdrant collection with an embedding vector and show the top matches with their payloads.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Qdrant Cloud Setup

steps.title: Create a Qdrant Cloud account
steps.description: Sign up at https://cloud.qdrant.io — the free tier includes a 1GB cluster, enough for testing. Self-hosted Qdrant works too (run it with an api-key configured).

steps.title: Create a cluster and API key
steps.description: Create a cluster, then open its API Keys tab and create a database API key. Copy it immediately — it is only shown once.

steps.title: Store the key and note your endpoint
steps.description: Add QDRANT_API_KEY=... to your .env file. Copy the cluster endpoint hostname from Cluster details — tools take it as the clusterHost parameter without scheme or port (e.g. xyz-example.eu-central-1-0.aws.cloud.qdrant.io); requests go to port 6333.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Collections tool with your clusterHost.

notes: The API key is sent in the api-key header; Qdrant Cloud also accepts Authorization: Bearer
Every tool needs the clusterHost parameter (hostname only; requests target HTTPS port 6333) — there is no global Qdrant API host
Vectors must match the collection's configured vector size; generate embeddings separately (e.g. with OpenAI or Mistral)
